title: Chamomile eye gel
subtitle: A soothing aloe vera and chamomile hydrosol gel for the delicate eye area
excerpt: This eye gel uses aloe vera as its base, cut with chamomile hydrosol and a small amount of glycerin. No fragrance or essential oil goes in: the skin around the eyes is thin and sensitive, and essential oils near the eyes cause irritation. A patch test before first use is essential with any new product near this area.
sourceNotes: Eye area product formulation. Aloe vera gel base: water-heavy, soothing, suitable for eye area. Chamomile hydrosol (Matricaria recutita): gentle floral water; no essential oils near eyes. Glycerin at 2% as humectant. No fragrance or essential oil. Broad-spectrum preservative at 0.5 to 1% required because aloe vera gel contains water. Patch test behind ear for 24 hours before use near eyes.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~30 g eye gel (fills a 30 ml glass jar or pump)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep preservative at 1% of total batch.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months at room temperature with preservative. Refrigerating extends to 4 months and makes the gel more soothing on application. Discard if the gel discolours or smells off.

glossaryTerms.definition: The clear inner gel from Aloe barbadensis leaves, or a cosmetic equivalent. Very high water content with polysaccharides that have a soothing, film-forming effect on skin. Suitable for sensitive and reactive skin.

Apply a small amount of a new product to the inside of the wrist or behind the ear and leave for 24 hours. If no redness, itching, or reaction occurs, the product is likely safe to use on the face. Always patch test products intended for the eye area.

content.text: forms the base of this eye gel. Chamomile hydrosol adds a light floral note and is well-tolerated by sensitive skin. No essential oil or fragrance is included: the skin around the eyes is thin and closely connected to mucous membranes. Essential oils near the eyes cause irritation. Always do a
before using any new product near the eye area.

items.prepNote: Clear cosmetic grade, not coloured or scented
Roman or German chamomile floral water; substitute distilled water if unavailable

content.text: Weigh all ingredients into the beaker. Stir gently until fully combined. Transfer to a clean glass jar with a lid. Label with the date.
Apply a pea-sized amount around the eye area using the ring finger, which exerts the lightest pressure. Pat gently; do not rub. Apply morning or evening after cleansing.
3 months at room temperature, 4 months refrigerated. Keep the lid closed. Discard if the colour changes or the gel smells off.
